_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Acute Sore Throat aligned to NHS Pharmacy First criteria with FeverPAIN-style scoring.
label: Sore Throat

questions.text: How long have symptoms been present?
Do you have difficulty breathing or trouble opening your mouth?
Have you had fever in the last 24 hours?
Can you see pus or white spots on your tonsils?
Do your tonsils or throat look severely inflamed?
Have you had a cough in the last 24 hours?
questions.clinicalNote: No cough scores one FeverPAIN point

questions.text: Have you had recurrent tonsillitis in the past year?
Have you had your tonsils removed (tonsillectomy)?
Are you immunocompromised (for example due to treatment or a long-term condition)?

redFlags.description: Airway compromise symptoms with sore throat
redFlags.message: Breathing difficulty with sore throat needs emergency care. Call 999 now.

redFlags.condition: q2 === true && q4 === true && q6 === true && q1 === 'More than 7 days'
redFlags.description: Severe inflammatory sore throat with prolonged symptoms
redFlags.message: Severe or persistent throat infection features need same-day urgent assessment.

redFlags.description: Immunocompromised with fever and sore throat
redFlags.message: Because you are immunocompromised and febrile, seek urgent same-day care.

eligibilityRules.reason: Previous tonsillectomy with recurrent throat symptoms should be reviewed by GP
Under 5 years old is outside Pharmacy First sore throat criteria

eligibilityRules.condition: age >= 5 && q3 === false && q8 === false && q9 === false && q10 === false
eligibilityRules.reason: Meets baseline Pharmacy First sore throat eligibility

outcomeRules.reason: Exclusion criteria or age threshold indicates GP assessment is required
High FeverPAIN score suggests likely bacterial infection requiring GP antibiotic assessment
Moderate FeverPAIN score is appropriate for pharmacy-led sore throat management
Low FeverPAIN score indicates likely viral sore throat; self-care advised
Needs clinician review where Pharmacy First criteria are not clearly met

selfCareAdvice: Rest, keep hydrated, and use simple pain relief if suitable. Most sore throats improve without antibiotics.
pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Throat lozenges / sprays for pain relief
Phenoxymethylpenicillin (where local Pharmacy First PGD criteria are met)
Alternative antibiotic options per local protocol
safetyNetAdvice: Seek urgent help immediately for breathing difficulty, inability to swallow fluids, severe worsening pain, or persistent high fever.
pathwayPatientDisclaimer: Community pharmacists may assess and treat acute sore throat for eligible patients under NHS Pharmacy First. FeverPAIN-style scoring supports triage but does not replace in-person clinical judgement.
